title: How do I allow students or staff to complete a self-check using Turnitin?
plain_text: The Turnitin plagiarism tool can be used to self-check written submissions and must be accessed through a Canvas course assignment. Instructors can add a Turnitin assignment to allow those enrolled as students to perform their plagiarism self-check for self-review and remediation in a registrar or manual course shell. If needed, you can submit a request for a non-credit, manual course to host a self-check Turnitin assignment. Import a Turnitin self-check assignment (non-graded) from Commons Turnitin is available through a Canvas course assignment for students listed on the People page in the course. For a registrar course, this includes the registered students for the course. For a manually enrolled course, this applies to those added to the course with the student role. To import a non-graded, self-check Turnitin assignment template from Canvas Commons into an existing registrar or manual enrollment course, complete the following: 1. Log in to Canvas. 2. Select Commons icon from the left-hand menu. 3. Once the Commons is opened, ensure the 'Northeastern University' filter is enabled, and then use the search bar to search for the keyword Turnitin. 4. Locate and select the 'Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU)' course assignment. 5. On the Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU), locate and select the 'Import/Download' link located on the right-hand side of the page. 6. From the 'Import/Download' slide-out panel, locate and select the course(s) in which you would like to add the Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU) assignment and select 'Import into Course' to initiate the import process. 7. Once the import is complete, you will see a green box that indicates success. 8. Open the course you imported the assignment into. The 'Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU)' assignment will be available in an 'Imported Assignments' group in the Assignments section of your course. 9. Edit the assignment title and information as needed to further customize the assignment for your course, as required. Request a new manual-enrolled course shell for plagiarism self-check A manual-enrolled course request can be submitted to create a non-credit, managed location for students, faculty, and staff to submit written works for Turnitin plagiarism review through a non-registrar course. This allows individuals such as research staff and students not enrolled in a related registrar course to have a location where they can self-submit written work, such as research reports or journal article drafts, for plagiarism review via Turnitin. Note: Students cannot be given official grades for manually enrolled courses. They can only be given grades for courses and enrollments generated by the Registrar. 1. To request a manual course, open the Request Canvas Sandbox, Development, Master Shell, or Manual Course form. 2. In the Course Shell Information field, select the 'Manual Course' option from the drop-down list provided. 3. Enter the desired title for the Turnitin Plagiarism course in the 'What is the name of your manual course?' field. 4. Verify that enrollment for this manual course will be managed manually by you or other course admins. Describe the audience and purpose of the course, including specification of use for Turnitin Plagiarism self-check submissions. 5. Use the drop-down provided to designate 'College Affiliation' for this manual, Turnitin Self-Check course shell. 6. Submit the request for processing. 7. Follow the guidance provided in the Importing a Turnitin Self-check Assignment (Non-graded) from Commons to import the Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU) assignment. 8. Add participants as students to the course. Refer to the article How do I add users to a course? in the Canvas Instructor Guide for more information, as needed. Additional resources What is Turnitin? How do I access the Turnitin Similarity Report panel in Canvas? How do I create a Turnitin Graded Assignment that allows multiple submissions? Help For questions about account or access errors, please email Academic Technologies Help and provide: Your name, The course in which you are using Turnitin, Where in the course you are accessing the Turnitin link, and The error message you are receiving.
body_html:
The Turnitin plagiarism tool can be used to self-check written submissions and must be accessed through a Canvas course assignment. Instructors can add a Turnitin assignment to allow those enrolled as students to perform their plagiarism self-check for self-review and remediation in a registrar or manual course shell. If needed, you can submit a request for a non-credit, manual course to host a self-check Turnitin assignment.

Table of contents

  • Import a Turnitin self-check assignment (non-graded) from Commons
  • Request a new manual-enrolled course shell for plagiarism self-check
  • Additional resources
  • Help

Import a Turnitin self-check assignment (non-graded) from Commons

Turnitin is available through a Canvas course assignment for students listed on the People page in the course. For a registrar course, this includes the registered students for the course. For a manually enrolled course, this applies to those added to the course with the student role. To import a non-graded, self-check Turnitin assignment template from Canvas Commons into an existing registrar or manual enrollment course, complete the following:

  • Log in to Canvas.
  • Select from the left-hand menu.
  • Once the Commons is opened, ensure the 'Northeastern University' filter is enabled, and then use the search bar to search for the keyword Turnitin.
  • Locate and select the 'Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU)' course assignment.
  • On the Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU), locate and select the 'Import/Download' link located on the right-hand side of the page.
  • From the 'Import/Download' slide-out panel, locate and select the course(s) in which you would like to add the Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU) assignment and select 'Import into Course' to initiate the import process.
  • Once the import is complete, you will see a green box that indicates success.
  • Open the course you imported the assignment into. The 'Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU)' assignment will be available in an 'Imported Assignments' group in the Assignments section of your course.
  • Edit the assignment title and information as needed to further customize the assignment for your course, as required.

Request a new manual-enrolled course shell for plagiarism self-check

A manual-enrolled course request can be submitted to create a non-credit, managed location for students, faculty, and staff to submit written works for Turnitin plagiarism review through a non-registrar course. This allows individuals such as research staff and students not enrolled in a related registrar course to have a location where they can self-submit written work, such as research reports or journal article drafts, for plagiarism review via Turnitin. Note: Students cannot be given official grades for manually enrolled courses. They can only be given grades for courses and enrollments generated by the Registrar.

  • To request a manual course, open the Request Canvas Sandbox, Development, Master Shell, or Manual Course form.
  • In the Course Shell Information field, select the 'Manual Course' option from the drop-down list provided.
  • Enter the desired title for the Turnitin Plagiarism course in the 'What is the name of your manual course?' field.
  • Verify that enrollment for this manual course will be managed manually by you or other course admins. Describe the audience and purpose of the course, including specification of use for Turnitin Plagiarism self-check submissions.
  • Use the drop-down provided to designate 'College Affiliation' for this manual, Turnitin Self-Check course shell.
  • Submit the request for processing.
  • Follow the guidance provided in the Importing a Turnitin Self-check Assignment (Non-graded) from Commons to import the Turnitin Student Draft Checker (NEU) assignment.
  • Add participants as students to the course. Refer to the article How do I add users to a course? in the Canvas Instructor Guide for more information, as needed.

Additional resources

  • What is Turnitin?
  • How do I access the Turnitin Similarity Report panel in Canvas?
  • How do I create a Turnitin Graded Assignment that allows multiple submissions?

Help

For questions about account or access errors, please email Academic Technologies Help and provide: Your name, The course in which you are using Turnitin, Where in the course you are accessing the Turnitin link, and The error message you are receiving.

Instructors can add a Turnitin assignment to allow those enrolled as students to perform their plagiarism self-check for self-review and remediation in a registrar or manual course shell.; Table of contents: Import a Turnitin self-check assignment (non-graded) from Commons, Request a new manual-enrolled course shell for plagiarism self-check, Additional resources, Help; Import a Turnitin self-check assignment (non-graded) from Commons: Turnitin is available through a Canvas course assignment for students listed on the People page in the course. To import a non-graded template from Commons, log in to Canvas, select the Commons icon, search for Turnitin, select the Student Draft Checker, and import into your course.; Request a new manual-enrolled course shell for plagiarism self-check: A manual-enrolled course request can be submitted to create a non-credit location for staff and students to self-submit work. Submit the Request Canvas form, select Manual Course, designate name and affiliation, and import the template assignment.; Additional resources: What is Turnitin?, How do I access the Turnitin Similarity Report panel in Canvas?, How do I create a Turnitin Graded Assignment that allows multiple submissions?; Help: For questions about account or access errors, please email Academic Technologies Help.
